title: Severe back pain and contractions in a 32-year-old at 34 weeks
presentation: A 32-year-old G2P1 female at 34 weeks gestation presents with sudden onset severe back pain, uterine contractions every 3 minutes, and vaginal bleeding described as dark red. She denies trauma. Vitals: BP 92/58, HR 138, RR 22, Temp 37.1C, SpO2 96%. Exam: uterus rigid and tender, fetal heart rate 90 bpm with late decelerations, cervix 4cm dilated.

gold_standard_workup.key_decision_point: Rigid painful uterus + dark vaginal bleeding + fetal distress = placental abruption until proven otherwise. Do NOT perform digital vaginal exam if placenta previa not excluded. Fetal bradycardia = emergency C-section. DIC is a major complication — check fibrinogen.
